meta.title: verbatra: keep your i18n locale files in sync, translate only what changed
meta.description: Keep your i18n locale files in sync. verbatra translates only the keys that changed, through your choice of AI or machine-translation provider. Start now.
meta.ogImageAlt: verbatra keeps your i18n locale files in sync and translates only the keys that changed.
meta.definition: verbatra is a CLI and SDK that keeps your i18n locale files in sync, translating only the keys that changed through your choice of AI or machine-translation provider.

hero.headline: Translate only what changed.
hero.lead: verbatra is a CLI and SDK that keeps your i18n locale files in sync. You maintain the source locale; it fills every other locale through the AI provider you choose.

compat.heading: One tool, every framework.
compat.body: verbatra reads the JSON locale formats your framework already uses, and fills them through the provider you choose.

showcase.heading: Watch it touch exactly one key.
showcase.body: A first run fills the new keys; change one source value and the next run sends only that key. Everything else is left untouched.

how.heading: A run is a short, predictable pipeline.

configure.title: Configure
configure.body: Point verbatra at your source locale, list the target languages, and pick a provider. Configuration lives in one file; API keys stay in your environment.

diff.title: Diff
diff.body: Each run compares your source locale against the committed lock file and sorts every key into new, changed, unchanged, or orphaned.

translate.title: Translate
translate.body: Only the new and changed keys are sent to your provider, in batches, with your glossary and tone applied. Everything else is left untouched.

verifyWrite.title: Verify & write
verifyWrite.body: Placeholder and ICU integrity are checked on every result. Anything that breaks is withheld; the rest is written to your locale files and the lock is updated.

why.heading: Built for teams that ship in many languages.

incremental.title: Incremental by default
incremental.body: A committed lock file records what was translated. Each run diffs the source and calls the provider only for the keys that changed.

provider.title: Your choice of provider
provider.body: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, or DeepL, chosen in one line of config. Switch providers without touching your locale files.

safety.title: Safe by construction
safety.body: Placeholder and ICU integrity are checked after every translation. A result that breaks a placeholder is withheld, never written.

environment.title: Keys stay in your environment
environment.body: Provider API keys are read from environment variables, never from the config file, never from CLI arguments, and never logged.

oneEngine.title: CLI and SDK, one engine
oneEngine.body: The verbatra command and @verbatra/sdk share a single orchestration core, so scripts, CI, and your own tooling behave identically.

dryRun.title: Dry runs and watch mode
dryRun.body: Preview exactly what a run would change without writing anything, or keep a watch running that translates as your source locale evolves.

avoidRetranslate.question: How does verbatra avoid re-translating everything on each run?
avoidRetranslate.answer: verbatra keeps a committed lock file that records what was already translated. On each run it diffs your source locale against that lock and sends only the new or changed keys to your provider; unchanged keys are left untouched.

providers.question: Which translation providers does verbatra support?
providers.answer: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, and DeepL. You choose one in a single line of config, and the API key is read from an environment variable, never from the config file.

icu.question: How does verbatra handle ICU placeholders and message formats?
icu.answer: It checks placeholder and ICU integrity after every translation. If a returned translation breaks a placeholder or produces invalid ICU, that result is withheld rather than written to your locale file.

formats.question: Which i18n file formats can verbatra read?
formats.answer: JSON formats for i18next, vue-i18n, next-intl, and ngx-translate, covering React, Vue, Next.js, Nuxt, Angular, and Node.js projects.

openSource.question: Can I preview a run before it writes anything?
openSource.answer: Yes. A dry run reports exactly which keys would be sent and written without touching your locale files, and watch mode keeps translating as your source locale changes.

ci.question: Is there an SDK as well as a CLI?
ci.answer: Yes. The verbatra command is a thin wrapper over @verbatra/sdk, which exposes the same translate and watch operations for scripts, CI, and your own tooling.

production.question: Is verbatra production-ready?
production.answer: verbatra is pre-1.0 and actively developed, so its API may still change before the 1.0 release; pin a version and read the changelog when you upgrade. What is already solid is the safety model: every run diffs against a committed lock file, checks placeholder and ICU integrity on each result, and withholds anything that breaks a placeholder rather than writing it to your locale file. Many teams run it in CI today; treat pre-1.0 as a signal to pin versions, not as a warning that results are unsafe.

footer.tagline: Translate only what changed. An open-source CLI and SDK that keeps your i18n locale files in sync through the provider you choose.
